title: How is this learning path structured

content.problemStatement:
Welcome to the Beginner's path to solving programming problems.
- Getting started, Basic math and Conditional statements are introductory modules.\
If you are well versed in these topics - feel free to skip to the module on [Logic building](https://www.codechef.com/learn/course/python-beginner-v2-p1/BP00BP12_V2/problems/LB00)
- Logic building covers our **5-step approach** to solving programming problems
- Array operations, String operations and Basic math continued will require patience, hard-work and heavy-lifting. If you want to directly start with these - click [here](https://www.codechef.com/learn/python-beginner-v2-p2)

How should you approach this learning path?
- Within each module - there are multiple topics. 
- You will be able to finish each topic in 30 - 45 mins
- Each topic consists of the following to ensure that you **Learn by doing**
  - Concept problems introduce new syntax, concepts and methods
  - Practice and Debugging problems require you to solve problems yourself
- At the end of each module, you have Review problems. 

Best of luck for your programming journey ahead! \
Click on 'Next' to proceed.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
